# expected-counts.txt — ranges, not exact values.
#
# Format: fixture   min_gadgets   max_gadgets
#
# Ranges chosen wide enough to absorb minor toolchain bumps
# (gcc 11→12, binutils N→N+1) but tight enough to catch a real
# regression in shrike's scanner. If a count falls outside the
# range on a CI runner, first check if the toolchain changed;
# if not, the scanner drifted and needs investigation.
#
# Measured baselines (as of 2026-04):
#   hello_x86_64   (ubuntu-24.04 gcc 13.2 -O2)    → ~42
#   hello_aarch64  (gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu 12.3)   → ~70
#   hello_riscv64  (gcc-riscv64-linux-gnu 13.2)   → ~58
#   hello_pe.exe   (mingw gcc 12.2 -O2)           → ~650
#
# PE is much bigger because mingw statically links the MSVCRT
# stubs and a small GCC runtime.

hello_x86_64    5    200
hello_aarch64   10   400
hello_riscv64   10   400
hello_pe.exe    50   3000
